rows.notes: Clear centered page title. No amounts present and lettering is crisp and unambiguous.
Clear heading directly beneath the title. Handwriting legible; no numeric columns present on this line.
Amount columns and digits (20 | 6 | 8) are well aligned with the right ruling lines and clearly written. Description text is legible.
Pounds '25' is clear in the pounds column; no shillings or pence marks appear in their columns. The place-name 'Newington' is reasonably clear though older script could be read slightly differently.
Numeric columns (33 | 6 | 8) are clearly aligned and pass validation. Surname 'Shiers' is legible though the abbreviation 'Dna' (Domina) is in old script.
Pounds '60' is clearly written in the pounds column; shillings and pence columns are blank. Description abbreviation 'ejusdem Dna' is readable though slightly stylized.
Full amount (30 | 9 | 2) is clean and well aligned. Short description 'Dno Rectori' is clear.
Amount (39 | 3 | 8) aligns with the ruling lines and reads clearly. 'Div Merton' and date '1843' are legible though written in older script.
Amount (39 | 3 | 8) is clear and matches the previous similar line. Description 'Id ad Fest. St. Michaelis' is readable.
Amounts (33 | 6 | 8) are well aligned and legible. Year '1844' visible; wording in Latin abbreviations clearly read.
Amount (45 | 11 | 3) is clear. The place 'Kidlington' is legible, though the ordinal '3ha' and 'anni parte' are in abbreviated script and slightly cramped.
Repeated line with a clear amount (30 | 9 | 2); digits are distinct and columns unambiguous.

rows.description: Stipend ad Fest D. Jo. Bapt. et Officiarius
rows.notes: Amount (19 | 13 | 4) is clearly written and aligns with ruling lines. Long description is readable though compressed.
Amount (33 | 6 | 8) is clear and matches earlier 'Dna Shiers' line. 'Idem' indicating same is legible.

rows.description: Augment Com ejusdem Dna Mod. et Subde
rows.notes: Pounds '56' is readable in pounds column; shillings/pence columns blank. Several abbreviated words in the description (e.g. 'Com', 'Mod.', 'Subde') are faded and partially ambiguous, so transcription of description is approximate.
Amount (15 | 3 | 9) aligns with ruling lines and is legible. 'Kidlington' and '4ta parte anni' readable though written in older script.
Clear repetition of Rectori line with amount (30 | 9 | 2). All digits unambiguous.

rows.description: Stipend Guinear ad fest St. Michaelis
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings (26 | 5) are readable; pence column blank (interpreted as 0). The name 'Guinear' is slightly uncertain in spelling but reasonably clear in context.
Amount (34 | 2 | 6) is well aligned and legible. 'Wittenham Longa' (longa/longa variation) is readable, though the scribe's long-hand slightly stylizes the final word.
Identical Rectori line with clear amount (30 | 9 | 2). 'ut supra' (as above) is readable.
Centered small header; text legible though the abbreviated '1 mi' is in old script and slightly compressed.

Amount (12 | 8 | 4) is clear and well aligned. Description is faded and the words are uncertain ('Aiorns ? ten parva' is a best-effort transcription of cramped, partially obscured script), hence lower confidence.
Final boxed total at bottom right (714 | 14 | 10) is clearly written and used as alignment reference for column detection. No description on this line.
